free_text: Hydrogen isotope double differential production cross
sections induced by 62.7 MeV neutrons on a lead
correction procedure by Monte Carlo simulation program
65 MeV proton beam strikes 0.3 mm thick
natural lithium target. Proton current is 10 micro-A,
Full width at half maximum is 2 MeV containing 50% of
neutrons,plus a flat continuum of low-energy neutrons.
telescopes were used simultaneously. Each of them
C3-H6 chamber for neutron flux monitoring
The time-of-flight method was deduced from the
time difference between the capacitive beam pickoff
and the delta-e detector. It was registered only
those events of charged particles detection which
are associated with neutrons in the main neutron
* Faraday cup collects the noninteracting protons

Corrections were made for thickness of the target
and for the effect of collimator
The systematic error is estimated to
the uncertainty of statistics in the H(n,p)
the uncertainty of solid angle corrections,
the uncertainty of number of target nuclei.
